name: dashboard-analytics
description: Dense analytics dashboard — tables, filters, empty states

design-brief.content:
Design a single-page operational dashboard for on-call engineers. Shows incident volume, MTTR trend, and a sortable recent incidents table. Primary job: scan health in under 10 seconds, then drill into one row.

Include: top summary KPI row (4 metrics), a simple trend strip (can be CSS-only chart metaphor), filters row (time range + severity), table with 8+ placeholder rows, and an empty state for the table when filters match nothing.

research-context.content: Users are interrupted and stressed; contrast and legibility beat decoration.

objectives-metrics.content: Reduce time to identify worst incident. Emphasize sort affordances and severity color system consistent with status.

design-constraints.content: Static HTML/CSS/JS. No chart libraries — use div-based or CSS gradients for trends. WCAG-minded contrast for severity badges.

strategy.name: Scanability under load
strategy.hypothesis: A fixed KPI band plus zebra table striping and sticky column headers will improve scan speed compared to a card-heavy layout, for fatigued on-call users.
strategy.rationale: Reduced eye travel and predictable grid alignment speed triage.
strategy.measurements: Rubric: table readable at 375px width; sticky header does not obscure KPIs; empty state explains how to clear filters.
